steps.title: Combine both halves
steps.content: The passage first says the 'akeke'e lives only on the Hawaiian archipelago and has adapted to a well-defined habitat. That specialization gives the species physical and behavioral traits that help it survive in that particular ecosystem.

steps.title: Include the consequence
steps.content: The word However introduces the important downside. Because the 'akeke'e is so specialized, it is especially vulnerable to environmental changes that disrupt the carefully balanced ecosystem where it lives. A main idea must include both specialization and vulnerability.

steps.title: Reject incomplete summaries
steps.content:
• Choice A overgeneralizes to Hawaiian birds as a group and misses the vulnerability.
  • Choice C adds unsupported claims about relatedness and shared habitat.
  • Choice D mentions specialization and location but leaves out the resulting susceptibility to habitat disturbance.
The answer is B.
